text.title: Demo Hypothesis Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Demo Hypothesis Template must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every demo session declares one PRIMARY hypothesis in the form 'If we showed customers X, they would react Y because Z'. Demos without a primary hypothesis are deferred until one is written.
rule.rationale: feature parades produce smile-and-nod feedback that is not signal.

rule.statement: Define a falsifier upfront: 'we would update the roadmap if we observe ___'. If no falsifier can be written, the hypothesis is unfalsifiable and the demo is rebooked.
Capture at least 3 verbatim reactions per customer. Paraphrased PM-notes do not count — the synthesizer needs raw quotes.

rationale.source: Anti-paraphrase rule from user-research literature (Erika Hall, Just Enough Rese

rule.rationale: Anti-paraphrase rule from user-research literature (Erika Hall, Just Enough Research).

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Customer demo / sneak-peek session Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
